comment: Spectral series. Hydrogen series (Lyman..Pfund) named for their lower principal level n_low; series limit = R_inf · (1/n_low²) corresponding to the n→infinity head. Series-limit wavelengths computed from the Rydberg formula 1/lambda = R(1/n_low^2) with R_H = 1.0967758e7 m^-1 (R_inf with reduced-mass correction for hydrogen). Na D doublet bundled as a fine-structure pair. Hg singlet + triplet series catalogued schematically (no closed-form limit; see notes).

primitives.name: Lyman series
primitives.notes: Lower level n=1 (ground). Series limit at the ionization threshold.

primitives.name: Balmer series
primitives.notes: Lower level n=2. Hα..Hδ are the visible workhorses of stellar spectroscopy.

primitives.name: Paschen series
primitives.notes: Lower level n=3. Near-IR; visible in dust-obscured nebulae.

primitives.name: Brackett series
primitives.notes: Lower level n=4. Mid-IR; JWST regime.

primitives.name: Pfund series
primitives.notes: Lower level n=5. Mid-IR; rarely observed individually.

primitives.name: Na D doublet
primitives.notes: Two-line fine-structure pair, not a Rydberg series. 3p→3s split by spin-orbit into ²P₃/₂ and ²P₁/₂. No closed-form series limit.

primitives.name: Hg intercombination (singlet↔triplet)
primitives.notes: Strong relativistic spin-orbit mixing makes the spin-forbidden 6³P₁→6¹S₀ at 253.65 nm one of mercury's brightest lines. Group label only — not a Rydberg series.
